acceptance_targets: [ch01-prose-001] The revised chapter contains no more than 4 instances of 'the way [X]' as a comparison/simile structure. Each remaining instance uses a different vehicle — no two share the same metaphor family (e.g., not two organic/natural analogies back-to-back). The proxy-office section (lines 49-105) and the evening domestic section (lines 115-225) each contain no more than 2.

[ch01-prose-002] The revised chapter uses 'the specific [noun]' and 'the particular [noun]' no more than 5 times combined. The line 107 sentence retains at most one instance of 'specific.' Each remaining instance is justified by a following clause that specifies what makes the thing specific or particular — the modifier does work beyond merely promising precision.

[ch01-prose-003] The revised chapter contains at most one 'not X, not Y, just/but Z' negation-then-definition structure. The closing passage (line 225) and the post-meeting passage (line 85) no longer share the same syntactic frame — they approach their respective emotional states through recognizably different sentence architectures.

[ch01-prose-004] The revised chapter contains at most one river/current metaphor. If a second metaphor replaces the removed instance, it uses a different vehicle and does not duplicate the tenor of invisible-force-organizing-behavior.

[ch01-craft-001] Dan's interior monologue between lines 139 and 225 includes at least one moment of unguarded register — a profane thought, an uncensored impulse, or an internal sentence fragment that breaks his observational composure — consistent with his style-bible profanity profile. The moment should feel like a crack in control, not a performance of rawness.

[ch01-prose-005] In the proxy-office section (lines 53-87), no more than 2 of 5 consecutive paragraphs use the trailing-clause architecture as their primary sentence shape. At least 3 of the cited passages (lines 7, 13, 53, 59, 87, 197) are restructured so that their primary syntactic movement is recognizably different from the trailing-clause default.
[fallback_full_award_contract] At least 1 full-book actionable finding cites snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1 and includes measurable acceptance criteria.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised chapter contains no more than 4 instances of 'the way [X]' as a comparison/simile structure. Each remaining instance uses a different vehicle — no two share the same metaphor family (e.g., not two organic/natural analogies back-to-back). The proxy-office section (lines 49-105) and the evening domestic section (lines 115-225) each contain no more than 2.
findings.evidence: The comparative construction 'the way [X]' appears 12+ times across the chapter. Representative instances: 'the way you might leave a cabinet open' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:3), 'the way signing things and nodding constituted his' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:19), 'the way the suit was, the way the commute was' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:53, two in one sentence), 'the way a current structures a river' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:105), 'the way you absorb a heartbeat' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:117), 'the way a river organizes its banks' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:123), 'the way he knew the radiator's knock' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:135), 'the way you say something when you're not sure' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:213). Additional instances at lines 31, 49, 115.
findings.problem: The 'the way [X]' comparison has become the chapter's default simile frame, appearing roughly once every 18 lines. The construction works well individually — it conveys Dan's habit of understanding his present through analogy — but at 12+ instances it becomes a predictable rhythmic signature. The reader begins anticipating the construction, which flattens the prose from observational to mannered. By the chapter's second half, each new 'the way' arrival feels less like narrative voice and more like autopilot.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'the way [X]' comparisons to no more than 4 total, distributed across the chapter's two scenes. In the proxy-office section (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:49-105), which currently contains 5+ instances, retain at most 2. In the evening domestic section (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:115-225), retain at most 2. For removed instances, use varied alternatives: direct rendering of the comparison without the 'the way' frame, sensory detail that makes the analogy implicit, or entirely different syntactic structures (apposition, embedded metaphor, physical action). The line 53 double use ('the way the suit was, the way the commute was') is the strongest candidate for retention because the repetition there is rhetorical rather than habitual.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised chapter uses 'the specific [noun]' and 'the particular [noun]' no more than 5 times combined. The line 107 sentence retains at most one instance of 'specific.' Each remaining instance is justified by a following clause that specifies what makes the thing specific or particular — the modifier does work beyond merely promising precision.
findings.evidence: 'The specific [noun]' appears 6 times: 'the specific hollow feeling' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:85), 'the specific pleasure...the specific weight...the specific satisfaction' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:107, three in one sentence), 'the specific slick glass feel' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:139), 'the specific emptiness' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:225). 'The particular [noun]' appears 6 times: 'the particular quality of a room' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:31), 'the particular late-February cold' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:33), 'a particular way of watching' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:71), 'a particular dialect of English' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:87), 'the particular dry precision' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:129), 'the particular quality of a child's world' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:185). Combined: 12 instances.
findings.problem: The precision-modifier construction 'the specific/particular [noun]' functions as a verbal tic rather than a narrative tool. The words promise exactitude but the actual precision comes from the nouns and their contexts, not from the modifier — 'the hollow feeling' is no less precise than 'the specific hollow feeling.' At 12 combined instances, the construction signals that the narrator reaches for the same grammatical handle whenever rendering interiority, which paradoxically makes the prose feel less precise by making every observation sound the same. Line 107, which uses 'specific' three times in one sentence ('the specific pleasure,' 'the specific weight,' 'the specific satisfaction'), concentrates the tic into a single passage that reads as stylistic compulsion.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce combined 'the specific [noun]' and 'the particular [noun]' to no more than 5 total. The line 107 triple (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:107) must retain at most one 'specific' — let the other two observations earn their precision through sensory detail rather than declaration. For removed instances, trust the nouns: 'the hollow feeling of having performed a function he couldn't name' is stronger than 'the specific hollow feeling.' Where genuine precision is needed, render it through concrete detail rather than announcing it with a modifier. Consider retaining 'specific/particular' only where the modifier creates a meaningful contrast — e.g., 'the particular quality of a room with one less body' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:31) where 'particular' earns its keep because the sentence goes on to specify what's particular.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised chapter contains at most one 'not X, not Y, just/but Z' negation-then-definition structure. The closing passage (line 225) and the post-meeting passage (line 85) no longer share the same syntactic frame — they approach their respective emotional states through recognizably different sentence architectures.
findings.evidence: Three instances of 'not X, not Y, just/but Z' negation-then-definition: (1) 'not dramatic, just persistent, the kind that found the gaps in your coat' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:33), (2) 'not sadness, not frustration, not even boredom exactly, but the specific hollow feeling of having performed a function he couldn't name' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:85), (3) 'not painful exactly, not unbearable, just empty, the specific emptiness of a container that had once held something and now held only the shape of what used to be there' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:225).
findings.problem: The negation-then-definition formula is used three times, and instances 2 and 3 are structurally near-identical: both open with 'not [emotion], not [emotion],' pivot on 'but/just,' and resolve into a 'the specific [noun]' construction. The first instance (line 33) describes weather; the second (line 85) describes Dan's post-meeting emptiness; the third (line 225) describes his end-of-day emotional state. Since instances 2 and 3 both describe varieties of the same feeling (ambient emotional emptiness) in the same syntactic frame, the chapter's two major reflective moments — the midday hollow and the final-page summary — mirror each other so closely they flatten into one observation repeated.
findings.rewrite_direction: Retain at most one instance of the 'not X, not Y, just Z' pattern. The strongest candidate for retention is line 85 (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:85), where the post-meeting emptiness is a new feeling Dan is trying to name and the negation-chain enacts his process of elimination. The closing passage (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:225) must use a different syntactic approach — render the emptiness through physical sensation, spatial detail, or a concrete image rather than through the same negation-chain. The weather instance (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:33) can be resolved by simply cutting 'not dramatic, just' and letting 'persistent' do the work: 'the persistent late-February cold of New York, the kind that found the gaps in your coat and stayed there.'

findings.acceptance_test: The revised chapter contains at most one river/current metaphor. If a second metaphor replaces the removed instance, it uses a different vehicle and does not duplicate the tenor of invisible-force-organizing-behavior.
findings.evidence: River/current metaphor used twice within 18 lines for the same idea: (1) 'structure his hours the way a current structures a river, not violently, not even visibly, just as the medium through which everything moved' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:105), (2) 'it had simply organized itself the way a river organizes its banks, through repetition and the path of least resistance' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:123).
findings.problem: Both instances use a river/current as vehicle for the same tenor: systems (AI scheduling at line 105, domestic habit at line 123) that organize human behavior through gradual, invisible force. The second arrival of the river image within 18 lines reads as the prose reaching for the same image because it worked the first time rather than because the second context demands it. The metaphor's specific content is also nearly identical — both emphasize non-violence, invisibility, and the natural/inevitable quality of the organizing force — so the reader gets the same insight twice in different wrappers.
findings.rewrite_direction: Remove or replace one of the two river metaphors. The line 105 instance (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:105) is the stronger candidate for retention because it describes AI scheduling, which is the chapter's central subject, and the extended elaboration ('not violently, not even visibly, just as the medium through which everything moved') is more fully realized. The line 123 instance (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:123), about the refrigerator shelf, can use a different vehicle — geological metaphor (sediment, erosion), gravitational metaphor, or simply render the habit directly without metaphor: 'though they'd never discussed this; it had organized itself through repetition.'

findings.acceptance_test: Dan's interior monologue between lines 139 and 225 includes at least one moment of unguarded register — a profane thought, an uncensored impulse, or an internal sentence fragment that breaks his observational composure — consistent with his style-bible profanity profile. The moment should feel like a crack in control, not a performance of rawness.
findings.evidence: Dan's interior monologue across the divorce scene (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:139-195) and the couch reflection (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:221-225) contains zero profanity, zero uncensored impulse, and no moment where his composure cracks internally. His style-bible profanity profile states: 'Moderate. Dan swears more when drinking or genuinely upset' with words like 'shit,' 'fuck,' 'goddamn.' In this scene, Dan is holding a beer (drinking) and learning his marriage is ending (genuinely upset), yet his interiority remains literary and composed: 'a relief so sudden that his grip on the beer bottle went slack' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:183), 'the dull fact that thirteen years and a child and a lease' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:183).
findings.problem: Dan's emotional register in the divorce scene is too clean for his character. His stress tell — noticing irrelevant physical details — is correctly deployed (the condensation on the beer bottle, line 139). But the tell is only half the profile: under genuine upset, Dan should also have moments of raw, unguarded internal register. The chapter renders his emotional response entirely through controlled literary observation, giving him the interiority of a narrator rather than a forty-four-year-old man whose wife just told him she's filing for divorce while he's holding a beer. The absence of any profane or uncensored thought across this and all other scenes makes his private register indistinguishable from his public one — he's performing composure even inside his own head. This is timidity: the prose retreats from the full character at the moment the scene most demands it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Insert at least one moment of unguarded internal register in the divorce scene (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:139-195). This does not mean a profanity-laden outburst — Dan's character is controlled. But a single internal 'shit' or 'fuck' that flickers through his mind before the composed observation resumes, or an unfinished internal sentence that his composure catches and redirects, would make his interiority feel inhabited rather than curated. The couch passage (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:221-225) is another candidate — Dan alone, processing, no audience for his composure, where a raw thought could surface briefly before the literary observation resumes. The key is contrast: one unguarded moment makes the surrounding composure feel like effort rather than nature.

findings.acceptance_test: In the proxy-office section (lines 53-87), no more than 2 of 5 consecutive paragraphs use the trailing-clause architecture as their primary sentence shape. At least 3 of the cited passages (lines 7, 13, 53, 59, 87, 197) are restructured so that their primary syntactic movement is recognizably different from the trailing-clause default.
findings.evidence: The chapter's dominant sentence architecture is [initial observation] followed by [trailing appositional/explanatory clause(s)]. Representative instances: 93-word sentence about Mira's hair with three trailing subordinate clauses (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:13), desk description with purpose-clause chain (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:59), document signing ceremony with cascading relative clauses (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:87), character introductions each defined by trailing relative clause (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:67), briefcase passage with three trailing 'the way' clauses (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:53), closing passage with cascading 'which was' clauses (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:197).
findings.problem: The trailing-clause construction is the chapter's gravitational sentence shape. In paragraphs 53, 59, 67, 87, and 197, the sentence opens with a noun or observation, then appends one or more defining/explaining clauses that tell the reader what to think about the initial noun. The effect is hypnotic and sometimes effective — it conveys the drift of proxy life — but when it becomes the default across office scenes, domestic scenes, and reflective passages alike, it stops being a tonal choice and becomes a structural habit. The style bible requires 'varied and responsive to scene pressure' rhythm and warns: 'Avoid settling into any single rhythmic default — if three consecutive paragraphs use the same sentence structure, one of them is wrong.' The proxy-office section (lines 53-87) has at least four consecutive paragraphs using this trailing-clause architecture.
findings.rewrite_direction: In at least 3 of the following passages, restructure the trailing-clause sentences: line 7 (Theo description), line 13 (Mira description), line 53 (briefcase passage), line 59 (desk description), line 87 (document ceremony), line 197 (city-at-evening passage). Options include: splitting into two shorter sentences where the defining clause becomes an independent observation; leading with the sensory/physical detail and appending the abstraction rather than the reverse; interrupting the clause chain with physical action, dialogue, or a paragraph break; varying subordination (front-loaded conditional or temporal clause instead of trailing relative). The proxy-office section (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:53-87) is the highest-priority target because its rhythmic monotony undercuts the comedy — absurd observations need varied rhythm to land as comic rather than catalogic.

findings.acceptance_test: At least 1 full-book actionable finding cites snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1 and includes measurable acceptance criteria.
findings.problem: Full-book review contract could not be validated; shortlist assessment is incomplete. Root validator reason: reviews/cycle_01/full_award.review.json finding #21 missing rewrite_direction
findings.rewrite_direction: Regenerate full-book findings with anchored references in snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1-400.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's risk zone is the gap between dignity and absurdity. The characters are adults with professional histories, a child, a mortgage — people with legitimate self-respect — who have been reduced to warm bodies in an optimization framework they cannot understand. The comedy must not protect them from the humiliation; the humiliation must not neutralize the comedy. Render both registers at full force, often in the same scene. The temptation will be to soften the comedy into gentle satire or to darken the humiliation into dystopian grimness — resist both. The novel's territory is the painful and the funny coexisting without one defusing the other. Commit to the specific physical embarrassment of being professionally irrelevant. Commit to the specific tenderness of parenting under surveillance. Commit to the specific grief of a marriage that might still be alive inside a system that has classified it as infrastructure. When in doubt, choose the version that makes you wince and then laugh, or laugh and then wince — both are correct.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure equivalent to a plot hole. When a scene requires the reader to sit with discomfort — economic shame, marital cruelty, parental failure, institutional humiliation, the specific indignity of being professionally redundant — render it with full specificity. 'They struggled with the situation' is not a sentence that belongs in this novel. Name the thing. Show the body. Let the reader be uncomfortable.
aesthetic_risk_policy.intimacy_and_desire_policy: Dan and Mira's residual physical attraction is a narrative force, not background radiation. When the novel's relational pressure calls for it, render desire with the same specificity as any other emotional state — where in the body it lives, how it changes the air in a room, the precise quality of proximity when two people who know each other's bodies are trying to pretend they don't. The almost-kiss in Chapter 7 should be as fully rendered as any confrontation scene.
aesthetic_risk_policy.register_commitment: This is a dark comedy, not a cautionary tale, not a dystopia, not a satire with a thesis. The novel should not argue about AI. It should render what it feels like to be a body in a world where bodies are overhead. The comic register is not a veneer over a serious message — it IS the message. A scene that is genuinely funny about genuine suffering is doing more literary work than a scene that is solemnly observant about the same material.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all spoken English in this novel. Uncontracted forms should be reserved for deliberate emphasis, Lena Morse's institutional register, or moments where a character is being carefully precise. If a line of dialogue uses 'do not' instead of 'don't' or 'I am' instead of 'I'm,' there should be a reason audible in the scene's pressure.
dialogue_rules.interruption_and_overlap_policy: Use interruptions, sentence fragments, and crosstalk when scene pressure warrants. Dan and Mira in particular should interrupt each other — they know each other's arguments before the sentences end. But every interruption must be motivated; do not scatter dashes for rhythmic texture alone.
dialogue_rules.leverage_shift_guidance: Every exchange of more than three lines should shift the power balance between speakers. This doesn't require overt conflict — a silence, a deflection, a piece of information withheld or accidentally revealed, a joke that lands wrong, a question left unanswered can all shift leverage. The dialogue must be going somewhere, not circling.
dialogue_rules.silence_as_dialogue: Silence, non-answers, and topic changes are valid dialogue moves and often more revealing than speech. When a character doesn't respond to something, the reader should feel the weight of what wasn't said. Render these moments through physical action or observed stillness, not by narrating that the character chose not to speak.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (single-scene compression vs. multi-scene chapters), dialogue density (some chapters are conversation-heavy, others are nearly silent), interiority depth (some chapters live mostly in the POV character's head, others are externally rendered), pacing (some chapters cover hours, others cover weeks in montage), and tonal register (comic chapters adjacent to devastating ones). The contrast between chapters is itself a narrative tool — use it to create whiplash, irony, and accumulating emotional force.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, precise, and register-sensitive. The prose absorbs the vocabulary of its setting: corporate language, tech terminology, bureaucratic formulas — but always filtered through the POV character's relationship to that language. Dan uses corporate words reflexively and with slight discomfort. Mira uses strategic vocabulary as native speech. The narrator should not editorialize on the language; let the words sit in context and let the reader hear the dissonance. Avoid: prestige-literary abstractions that haven't been earned by sensory grounding, and journalistic explainer-prose that breaks character to tell the reader about the world.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating between Dan and Mira as primary POV characters. The camera stays tight to the current POV character's perceptions and vocabulary — what Dan notices is different from what Mira notices. In shared-POV chapters, the rotation should be clear (section breaks or sustained passages in one POV before shifting) rather than head-hopping within a scene.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Short declarative sentences for comic timing, for shock, and for moments of clarity. Longer, more syntactically complex sentences for interiority, sensory immersion, and emotional complexity. The rhythm should accelerate during confrontation and slow during reflection. Avoid settling into any single rhythmic default — if three consecutive paragraphs use the same sentence structure, one of them is wrong.
